title: RNAlater Recipe
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"32i5s","text":"This is a storage solution that stabilizes and protectsRNA while inactivating RNase","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"30dlj","text":"Note: If you read the patent application, they refer to an ammonium sulfate concentration of 700g per L. What they really mean is add 700g to 1L (including EDTA and sodium citrate at conc. listed above), so you end up with more than 1L total volume. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":5}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9thoa","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2i5j7","text":"Note: The pH should be ~7 using this recipe. The patent states that pH can range from 4-8; the preferred commercial pH is 5.2; I use pH 7 for bacterioplankton samples, but pH can be lowered by adding H2SO4.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":5},{"style":"sub","offset":201,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":204,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9j0l5","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9kvil","text":"Note:  Since this is a storage solution for RNA, use high quality reagents (\"molecular biology\" grade) when preparing the buffer.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":5}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
creator: Bonnie Poulos
